problems.matrix_str:
from sympy import Matrix, diag, eye, sqrt
v1 = Matrix([1, 0, -1])
v2 = Matrix([0, 1, 1])
v3 = Matrix([1, 1, 0])
lambda1, lambda2, lambda3 = 4, -3, 6
# Gram-Schmidt process to orthogonalize
q1 = v1 / v1.norm()
u2 = v2 - (v2.dot(q1)) * q1
q2 = u2 / u2.norm()
u3 = v3 - (v3.dot(q1)) * q1 - (v3.dot(q2)) * q2
q3 = u3 / u3.norm()
Q = Matrix.hstack(q1, q2, q3)
D = diag(lambda1, lambda2, lambda3)
A = Q * D * Q.T
is_orthogonal = (Q.T * Q).simplify() == eye(3)
result = {'A': A, 'is_symmetrical': A == A.T, 'is_orthogonal': is_orthogonal, 'eigenvalues': A.eigenvals()}
